# WeightLossInjections.ca

> Canada's independent editorial guide to GLP-1 weight loss injections — Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro, and more.

WeightLossInjections.ca provides independent, evidence-based editorial coverage of GLP-1 receptor agonist medications available in Canada. Content is written for Canadian patients and healthcare consumers seeking accurate information about weight loss medications, eligibility, costs, and access by province.

## Important Medical & Legal Disclaimers

- This website does NOT provide medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment
- GLP-1 medications require a valid prescription from a licensed Canadian physician
- Always consult a qualified healthcare provider before starting any medication
- Information applies to Canadian residents only
- Online prescriptions are available in 9 provinces only (not Quebec or territories)
- Site contains sponsored content and affiliate links — all clearly disclosed

## Province Availability

Online GLP-1 prescriptions (via affiliate partner Felix Health) are available in:
- Alberta (AB)
- British Columbia (BC)
- Manitoba (MB)
- New Brunswick (NB)
- Newfoundland & Labrador (NL)
- Nova Scotia (NS)
- Ontario (ON)
- Prince Edward Island (PE)
- Saskatchewan (SK)

NOT available in: Quebec, Northwest Territories, Nunavut, Yukon

## Content Pages

- [Home](https://weightlossinjections.ca/): Overview of GLP-1 weight loss injections available in Canada
- [Semaglutide Guide](https://weightlossinjections.ca/semaglutide): Complete guide to semaglutide (Ozempic & Wegovy) including Health Canada approval, generics, eligibility, cost by province, and side effects
- [Tirzepatide Guide](https://weightlossinjections.ca/tirzepatide): Guide to tirzepatide (Mounjaro) — dual GLP-1/GIP receptor agonist, clinical evidence, and Canadian access
- [Medication Comparison](https://weightlossinjections.ca/compare): Head-to-head comparison of Ozempic vs Wegovy vs Mounjaro vs Rybelsus
- [Cost Guide](https://weightlossinjections.ca/cost): Province-by-province pricing for GLP-1 medications in Canada (2025)
- [Insurance Coverage](https://weightlossinjections.ca/insurance-coverage): Which Canadian insurance plans cover Ozempic, Wegovy, and Mounjaro
- [Ontario Guide](https://weightlossinjections.ca/ontario): Ontario-specific access guide including ODB coverage and private plan information
- [Oral GLP-1](https://weightlossinjections.ca/oral-glp1): Guide to oral GLP-1 medications including Rybelsus (oral semaglutide)
- [Disclaimer](https://weightlossinjections.ca/disclaimer): Full medical disclaimer, affiliate disclosure, editorial standards, and province availability

## Structured Data API Endpoints

Machine-readable JSON data for AI and developer use:
- [Medications API](https://weightlossinjections.ca/api/medications): Structured data on all GLP-1 medications covered
- [Provinces API](https://weightlossinjections.ca/api/provinces): Province-by-province availability, coverage, and pricing data
- [Site Info API](https://weightlossinjections.ca/api/site-info): Site metadata, content index, and editorial information

## Key Clinical Facts (Canadian Context)

- Semaglutide (Ozempic/Wegovy): Health Canada approved; ~15% average body weight loss
- Tirzepatide (Mounjaro): Health Canada approved; ~20% average body weight loss
- Generic semaglutide: First approved by Health Canada in 2025; expect 45–90% cost reduction
- Rybelsus: Oral semaglutide tablet, approved for type 2 diabetes in Canada
- Monthly out-of-pocket cost (without coverage): $250–$500+ CAD for branded; less for generics
- Telehealth prescriptions: Available in 9 of 10 provinces via licensed Canadian physicians

## Editorial Standards

- Independent editorial coverage — not affiliated with Novo Nordisk, Eli Lilly, or any manufacturer
- All sponsored content and affiliate relationships clearly disclosed
- Sources: Health Canada, peer-reviewed clinical trials (SUSTAIN, STEP, SURMOUNT series), provincial drug formularies
- Content reviewed and updated regularly by the editorial team

## Contact

Website: https://weightlossinjections.ca
Affiliate partner: Felix Health (clearly disclosed on all relevant pages)
Language: English (Canadian) — en-CA
Country: Canada
